name: [BUG-004] Username has no maximum length validation
description:
Validation gap: POST /api/auth/register accepts usernames of any length.
Industry standard is a maximum of 50-100 characters. Accepting 500+
character usernames wastes database storage, can break UI display
elements, and may be exploited for denial-of-service through repeated
registrations with huge payloads.

Expected: 400 Bad Request when username exceeds maximum allowed length.
Actual:   201 Created — 500-character username is stored successfully.
descriptionHtml:
Validation gap: POST /api/auth/register accepts usernames of any length.
Industry standard is a maximum of 50-100 characters. Accepting 500+
character usernames wastes database storage, can break UI display
elements, and may be exploited for denial-of-service through repeated
registrations with huge payloads.

Expected: 400 Bad Request when username exceeds maximum allowed length.
Actual: 201 Created — 500-character username is stored successfully.
statusMessage:
XFAIL BUG-004: POST /api/auth/register has no maximum length validation on username — accepts up to 500+ chars

AssertionError: Expected 400 for username with 500 characters but got 201. No maximum length is enforced.
assert 201 == 400
 +  where 201 = <WrapperTestResponse 621 bytes [201 CREATED]>.status_code
statusTrace:
tests\api\test_known_failures.py:117: in test_register_username_max_length_not_validated
    assert res.status_code == 400, (
E   AssertionError: Expected 400 for username with 500 characters but got 201. No maximum length is enforced.
E   assert 201 == 400
E    +  where 201 = <WrapperTestResponse 621 bytes [201 CREATED]>.status_code
